hey so lets add this agentic workflow to the measuring ai proficiency project as it already has agentic workflows setup and env virables and keys setup 
I want to setup an end to end cloud agent workflow in github agentic workflows make a plan for me https://github.github.com/gh-aw/?utm_source=blog-agentic-workflows-cta&utm_medium=blog&utm_campaign=agentic-workflows-tech-preview-feb-2026 Welcome to Peli's Agent Factory | GitHub Agentic Workflows https://github.github.com/gh-aw/blog/2026-01-12-welcome-to-pelis-agent-factory/ https://github.com/github/gh-aw and https://github.com/github/gh-aw-actions go through all the pages an research, i want to setup a proof of concept agent factory

Apr 14
Before I dig in, three quick things to aim the research properly:

Agent runtime: Claude Code, Copilot, Codex CLI, or "show me all options and recommend one"?
PoC scope: single repo sandbox to learn the mechanics, or something closer to a TV 2 platform-team use case (e.g., auto-triage issues, doc updates, IaC drift checks)?
Secrets/MCP: do you want the PoC to include MCP server wiring (Atlassian, Slack, etc.) from day one, or keep it clean and add those in a second pass?
Run time is GitHub Copilot agentic workflows go trough all the references i gave you and it will be obvious

Apr 14
Got it, diving into gh-aw, the Agent Factory post, and the actions repo now to build you a concrete PoC plan.


GitHub agentic workflows research



Research complete
•
256 sources
•
10m 9s
Your GitHub Agentic Workflows PoC plan for TV 2 Denmark is ready. It covers the full architecture and security model, prerequisites and setup steps, a hello-world workflow plus three progressively advanced examples (issue triage, daily status reports, and PR review assistance), Copilot runtime configuration, safe outputs reference, debugging and iteration techniques, key metrics to track, known gotchas, and a concrete roadmap for scaling from PoC to organization-wide rollout with an Agent Factory pattern.

## Overview

Create a pull request for the changes in this session.

## Steps

1. Check for uncommitted changes. If any exist, stage and commit them with a message that describes what changed and why.
2. Push the branch to the remote.
3. Open a PR with `gh pr create`. Write a title that summarizes the change and a body that covers what changed, why, and anything a reviewer should know. If the repo has a PR template, follow it.
4. Report the PR URL when done. Wrap the URL in a `<pr-created>` tag on its own line so the UI can render a live status card, like this: `<pr-created>https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123</pr-created>`

If a PR already exists for this branch, push any new commits and report the existing URL wrapped in the same `<pr-created>` tag instead of creating a duplicate.

task: ok

### Key Insight

Your current setup covers the **specification → implementation → review → learning** loop well (spec-refiner → implementation → reviewer → self-improvement-meta). What's missing is the **triage, test quality, and continuous refactoring** middle layers that keep a maturing codebase healthy between planning cycles. The gh-aw repo is essentially showing you the "layer 2" automation that turns a well-planned project into a well-maintained one.</result>

task:
### Priority Order for Implementation

**Tier 1 (High impact, 1-2 weeks each):**
1. Issue triage agent (daily, auto-label issues)
2. PR triage agent (categorize agent PRs by risk/type)
3. Daily test improver (find test gaps)

**Tier 2 (Medium impact, follow Tier 1):**
4. Code simplifier (refactor merged code)
5. Daily doc updater (keep docs in sync)
6. CI coach (optimize workflow performance)

**Tier 3 (Nice to have, lower priority):**
7. Code scanning fixer (automate security fixes)
8. Duplicate code detector (consolidate utilities)

6. CI coach (optimize workflow performance)

8. Duplicate code detector (consolidate utilities)

<result>Perfect. Now I have enough information to generate the gap analysis. Let me compile my findings:

## Gap Analysis: AI Proficiency Project vs. gh-aw Reference & Agentics Sample

### What You Already Have (5 Core Workflows)

1. **spec-refiner.md** - Spec planning (issue labeled `needs-spec` → structured plan file)
2. **reviewer.md** - Plan-aware PR review with calibration by implementer agent
3. **self-improvement-meta.md** - Nightly extraction of learnings from workflow failures
4. **ai-proficiency-pr-review.md** - Custom PR assessment of AI proficiency maturity
5. **ai-proficiency-weekly-report.md** - Weekly trends tracking for proficiency levels

**Plus two in-flight (not yet on main):**
- **ci-cleaner.md** - CI failure recovery with auto-fix
- **contribution-checker.md** - PR evaluation against CONTRIBUTING.md

---

### What's Missing & Would Be Useful

These patterns from gh-aw and agentics sample packs directly align with Python CLI + agentic workflow goals:

#### 1. **Issue & PR Triage Agents** (High Value)
- **issue-triage-agent** (gh-aw) / **issue-triage** (agentics) - Daily labeling of unlabeled issues by type
- **pr-triage-agent** (gh-aw) - Periodic categorization and risk assessment of open agent PRs
- **Rationale**: You don't have any automated triage. These run daily/6-hourly and organize incoming work by severity/type. Critical for coordinating multiple agentic workflows.

#### 2. **Quality & Testing Automation** (Medium-High Value)
- **test-quality-sentinel** (gh-aw) - Analyzes test implementation beyond coverage (happy-path only, implementation details, duplication)
- **daily-test-improver** (agentics) - Finds test gaps a
